displayName: CFG-CA-001 — Require MFA and Compliant Device for All CFG Applications
description: Core ZTA Conditional Access policy for Crestview Financial Group. Requires MFA and device compliance for all users accessing any CFG application. No network location exceptions — enforces ZTA principle that location is not a trust signal.

applications.zta_note: All applications included — ZTA principle: no application is implicitly trusted

locations.zta_note: All locations included — network location is NOT a trust signal under ZTA

grantControls.zta_note: Both MFA AND device compliance required — neither alone is sufficient

signInFrequency.zta_note: Re-authentication required every hour — no persistent implicit trust

persistentBrowser.zta_note: Persistent browser sessions disabled — every session must be verified

additionalPolicies.description: Legacy authentication protocols do not support MFA and must be blocked entirely. Legacy auth is a common attack vector — blocking it is a prerequisite for effective ZTA.
additionalPolicies.zta_note: Legacy protocols blocked — cannot enforce MFA on legacy auth, therefore it must be eliminated

additionalPolicies.displayName: CFG-CA-003 — Privileged Role MFA + PIM Enforcement
additionalPolicies.description: All privileged directory roles require MFA at every sign-in with no exception. Privileged Identity Management (PIM) enforces JIT activation for all admin roles.
additionalPolicies.zta_note: Privileged accounts face the highest risk — strongest controls applied. PIM ensures no standing admin privilege.

additionalPolicies.displayName: CFG-CA-004 — High User Risk Requires Password Change
additionalPolicies.description: When Identity Protection detects a high-risk user (e.g. credentials found in breach data), the user is required to change their password before access is restored.
additionalPolicies.zta_note: Compromised identity is immediately contained — risk-based response prevents credential abuse

regulatory_alignment.OSFI_B13: Conditional Access provides the governance and identification layer required by B-13 Principles 3 and 14 — continuous access verification with risk-based controls
regulatory_alignment.OSFI_E21: Blocking legacy auth and requiring device compliance reduces the attack surface available to adversaries — directly supporting operational resilience objectives
regulatory_alignment.PIPEDA: MFA and device compliance requirements reduce the risk of unauthorized access to customer PII through credential compromise
regulatory_alignment.FINTRAC: Privileged role enforcement and session controls prevent unauthorized modification of AML transaction records
